query: My cousin invited me to a family event and I feel like nobody wants me there.

anchors.label: Alex's cousin invited him to attend his upcoming wedding.
anchors.text:
Type: Situation Statement
Label: Alex's cousin invited him to attend his upcoming wedding.
Layer: dataset_grounded
Text: Alex's cousin invited him to attend his upcoming wedding.
Source cases: 1-1
Triggers thoughts: It will be stressful and negative; people will ask me questions I cannot answer

anchors.label: Not being invited by a friend for a dinner with other friends.
anchors.text:

Source cases: 43-2
Triggers thoughts: My friends don't want me around, I am forgotten and excluded.

anchors.label: It will be stressful and negative; people will ask me questions I cannot answer
anchors.text:
Type: Automatic Thought Statement
Label: It will be stressful and negative; people will ask me questions I cannot answer 
Layer: dataset_grounded
Text: It will be stressful and negative; people will ask me questions I cannot answer or don't want to answer; my mum would be ashamed of me and critical of me as always; They don't want me there anyway, nobody likes me in this family.
Source cases: 1-1
Associated topics: Stress overload
Evokes emotions: anxious, worried, fearful, scared, tense, sad, down, lonely, unhappy
Influences behaviors: Ignored the invitation and did not respond to the RSVP request. Ignored phone ca

anchors.label: Family interactions will inevitably lead to judgment; any engagement with family
anchors.text:
Type: Automatic Thought Statement
Label: Family interactions will inevitably lead to judgment; any engagement with family
Layer: dataset_grounded
Text: Family interactions will inevitably lead to judgment; any engagement with family members will be uncomfortable; they will belittle me.
Source cases: 32-1
Evokes emotions: anxious, worried, fearful, scared, tense, disappointed
Influences behaviors: Tries to think of excuses to not show up; purposefully ignores texts from family

history.label: The patient has a history of substance abuse and has been through rehab to overc
history.text: The patient has a history of substance abuse and has been through rehab to overcome it. He has had issues with his family, particularly with his mother, where he has felt rejected and emotionally neglected. He has struggled with obesity since childhood, which has affected his self-esteem and body image. He also has a history of being victimized and bullied due to his weight.
history.canonical_text: The patient has a history of substance abuse and has been through rehab to overcome it. He has had issues with his family, particularly with his mother, where he has felt rejected and emotionally neglected. He has struggled with obesity since childhood, which has affected his self-esteem and body image. He also has a history of being victimized and bullied due to his weight.

intermediate_beliefs.label: Helpless: I'm just not very good at handling stress and I have poor self-control
intermediate_beliefs.text: Helpless: I'm just not very good at handling stress and I have poor self-control, which is why I need to not put myself in stressful situations. Unlovable: If I show my true self, people will reject me the way my mother rejected me.
intermediate_beliefs.canonical_text: Helpless: I'm just not very good at handling stress and I have poor self-control, which is why I need to not put myself in stressful situations. Unlovable: If I show my true self, people will reject me the way my mother rejected me.

intermediate_beliefs.label: Helpless: There's nothing I can do to change my situation. I cannot control myse
intermediate_beliefs.text: Helpless: There's nothing I can do to change my situation. I cannot control myself. Unlovable: I don't deserve to be happy, so why even try to stay clean?
intermediate_beliefs.canonical_text: Helpless: There's nothing I can do to change my situation. I cannot control myself. Unlovable: I don't deserve to be happy, so why even try to stay clean?

coping_strategies.label: The patient has adopted avoidance as a coping strategy by distancing himself fro
coping_strategies.text: The patient has adopted avoidance as a coping strategy by distancing himself from his family to reduce exposure to negativity and conflict. He has started scheduling pleasant activities and planning his day ahead of time in order to maintain a sense of control over his emotions and circumstances.
coping_strategies.canonical_text: The patient has adopted avoidance as a coping strategy by distancing himself from his family to reduce exposure to negativity and conflict. He has started scheduling pleasant activities and planning his day ahead of time in order to maintain a sense of control over his emotions and circumstances.

history.label: The client has had experiences of feeling unheard and emotionally invalidated si

intermediate_beliefs.label: People will not find me worth their time. If someone does not reach out to me, i
intermediate_beliefs.text: People will not find me worth their time. If someone does not reach out to me, it means they do not value me. If I am not explicitly included, I am unwanted. My worth is contingent on others' acceptance.

intermediate_beliefs.text: When I am depressed, no one will want to be around me. If I'm not perfect, people will reject me. My value to friends is conditional on my ability to be socially available and upbeat.

coping_strategies.text: The client uses avoidance like declining invitations or rebuffing attempts to interact (such as Edson's dinner invitation) as a protective measure. They seek affirmation from others to combat feelings of rejection but also distance themselves from situations where they could potentially feel hurt.

history.label: The client has a history of complicated family dynamics and struggles with relat
history.text: The client has a history of complicated family dynamics and struggles with relationships within his family. He expresses feelings of disconnection and discomfort around his parents and feels slighted or judged by them, leading to detachment. Past issues related to his mother's illness and his parents' support of his education have generated feelings of guilt and frustration. The client also hints at possible issues with his mother's behavior towards him and possibly others (mentions of her being 'broken' and a past affair).
history.canonical_text: The client has a history of complicated family dynamics and struggles with relationships within his family. He expresses feelings of disconnection and discomfort around his parents and feels slighted or judged by them, leading to detachment. Past issues related to his mother's illness and his parents' support of his education have generated feelings of guilt and frustration. The client also hints at possible issues with his mother's behavior towards him and possibly others (mentions of her being 'broken' and a past affair).

intermediate_beliefs.label: I must be acknowledged and recognized by my parents to demonstrate my worth. [du
intermediate_beliefs.text: I must be acknowledged and recognized by my parents to demonstrate my worth. [during depression] My parents see me as incompetent and a failure. My accomplishments can never get acknowledged by them, therefore I'm worthless and failing.

coping_strategies.label: The client copes by detaching from his family, cutting off relationships when fe
coping_strategies.text: The client copes by detaching from his family, cutting off relationships when feeling slighted, and trying to maintain control over his life and interactions. He also has a tendency to 'test' relationships through causing hurt, to validate his personal significance and the authenticity of the connection.

intermediate_beliefs.label: I must meet others' expectations. Expressing weaknesses or asking for help is fr
intermediate_beliefs.text: I must meet others' expectations. Expressing weaknesses or asking for help is frowned upon and not beneficial. I need to have a facade of control or a performance to be accepted or to interact socially. Discussing problems is pointless and signs of weakness must be hidden, otherwise nobody likes me and I will be abandoned.
intermediate_beliefs.canonical_text: I must meet others' expectations. Expressing weaknesses or asking for help is frowned upon and not beneficial. I need to have a facade of control or a performance to be accepted or to interact socially. Discussing problems is pointless and signs of weakness must be hidden, otherwise nobody likes me and I will be abandoned.

intermediate_beliefs.label: If I'm failing to meet expectations, or showing my vulnerability and weakeness,
intermediate_beliefs.text: If I'm failing to meet expectations, or showing my vulnerability and weakeness, people will know I'm just a weak person and a failure. I will eventually be unwanted and abandoned.

coping_strategies.label: The patient uses avoidance (e.g., not attending gatherings), suppression of emot
coping_strategies.text: The patient uses avoidance (e.g., not attending gatherings), suppression of emotions, and intellectualization as coping methods. He also engages in counterproductive self-talk, pseudo-performances in social interactions, and rationalization to avoid showing vulnerability. He also uses grandiosity and masculinity to cover up his insecurities.

coping_strategies.label: The patient turns to substances as a coping strategy to escape his feelings. He
coping_strategies.text: The patient turns to substances as a coping strategy to escape his feelings. He has been staying at his girlfriend's place to avoid his parents' influence and the associated stress. He also uses yelling and stepping away from situations to manage his low self esteem.

target.label: Loss Of Control
target.description: Belief that urges, emotions, or behavior cannot be contained.

target.label: Helplessness
target.description: Sense of weakness, incompetence, entrapment, or inability to cope.

target.label: Avoidance Rule
target.description: Rule that avoidance is necessary to stay safe or cope.

target.label: Control Rule
target.description: Rule that strict control is necessary to prevent collapse or harm.

target.label: Control Rule
target.description: Rule that strict control is necessary to prevent collapse or harm.

target.label: Hopelessness Rule
target.description: Assumption that change is futile or effort will fail.

target.label: Avoidance
target.description: Staying away from people, places, feelings, or tasks.

target.label: Escape
target.description: Using distraction, fantasy, or numbing to leave distress.

target.label: Substance Use Coping
target.description: Using substances to alter mood, avoid pain, or escape.

target.label: Anger
target.description: Frustration, resentment, irritation, or rage.

target.label: Irritability
target.description: Low frustration tolerance, agitation, or touchiness.

target.label: Anxiety
target.description: Fear, worry, dread, or threat sensitivity.

target.label: Fear
target.description: Immediate threat, alarm, or acute vulnerability.

target.label: Relapse Behavior
target.description: Returning to addictive or high-risk coping patterns.

prompt_context:
Retrieved hybrid graph context:
- Top matched nodes: Alex's cousin invited him to attend his upcoming wedding. (situation_statement), Not being invited by a friend for a dinner with other friends. (situation_statement), It will be stressful and negative; people will ask me questions I cannot answer  (automatic_thought_statement), Family interactions will inevitably lead to judgment; any engagement with family (automatic_thought_statement), Being invited to visit his sister but currently being financially unstable. (situation_statement)
- Candidate case matches:
  - Alex 1-1 (score=0.4879) via Alex's cousin invited him to attend his upcoming wedding.
  - Skyler 43-2 (score=0.4256) via Not being invited by a friend for a dinner with other friends.
  - Leo 32-1 (score=0.4115) via Family interactions will inevitably lead to judgment; any engagement with family
- Top case profile: Alex 1-1
- Situation examples: Alex's cousin invited him to attend his upcoming wedding.
- Automatic thought examples: It will be stressful and negative; people will ask me questions I cannot answer or don't want to answer; my mum would be ashamed of me and critical of me as always; They don't want me there anyway, nobody likes me in this family.
- Emotion examples: anxious, worried, fearful, scared, tense | sad, down, lonely, unhappy
- Behavior examples: Ignored the invitation and did not respond to the RSVP request. Ignored phone calls from family.
- Possible history: The patient has a history of substance abuse and has been through rehab to overcome it. He has had issues with his family, particularly with his mother, where he has felt rejected and emotionally neglected. He has struggled with obesity since childhood, which has affected his self-esteem and body image. He also has a history of being victimized and bullied due to his weight.
- Possible core beliefs: I am out of control. | I am trapped.
- Possible intermediate beliefs: Helpless: I'm just not very good at handling stress and I have poor self-control, which is why I need to not put myself in stressful situations. Unlovable: If I show my true self, people will reject me the way my mother rejected me. | Helpless: There's nothing I can do to change my situation. I cannot control myself. Unlovable: I don't deserve to be happy, so why even try to stay clean?
- Possible coping: The patient has adopted avoidance as a coping strategy by distancing himself from his family to reduce exposure to negativity and conflict. He has started scheduling pleasant activities and planning his day ahead of time in order to maintain a sense of control over his emotions and circumstances.
